Find Sherpas Boutique Search Agency
Search Quality Report

60 Manufactum Searches. 33 Revenue-Risk Moments.

An independent audit of how Manufactum search handles product names, brands, filters, and shopper intent.
Prepared by Michal Pekarcik · Find Sherpas

A sample of the 60 real customer queries we tested

Fermob blaue Gartenstühle Berg Fermb Gartenmöbel Balkon einrichten Sommer Gartenmöbel Garten möbel Bergs Poter Vogelhaus gartenmöbel Koffer CHICO Hängematten Ferm Gießkanne Doppler vogelschutz Metallwaren metallw renner Töpfe Küche Körperpflege Küche-Zubehör Garten Bergs Potter Hänge matten Bergs Potter große Töpfe ARCALO
Report Crossroads

Jump to a section

Search risk at a glance → Tested query patterns → Improvement map → Advanced diagnostics → Prioritized roadmap → Revenue-risk failures → Detailed analysis → Complete test results → Scope and boundaries →
Summary

Search risk at a glance

The short version

We tested 60 realistic customer queries against Manufactum's search, live on site. The audit checks whether customers see a relevant top result, not just whether the search engine returns something.

This report focuses on the 33 queries where a customer likely hits a dead end: an off-topic result, a missing interpretation, a dropped constraint, or a relevant product buried too far down.

The first fix to ship: Implement brand name boosting for exact brand matches — This fixes Product Discovery and Brand & Model Search by ensuring products containing the exact brand name in queries rank at the top regardless of other factors.

55%
revenue risk. 33 of 60 tested queries cause a likely customer bounce today. These are queries where a customer sees a dead end: no relevant top result, wrong interpretation, or the right product buried too far down.
33
Revenue risk
Off-topic top result, missing interpretation, or severe ranking failure. The customer leaves.
22
Friction
The query is close, but still creates work: the best match is not surfaced cleanly enough.
5
Working well
Top result is relevant and ranking is near-optimal. No immediate attention needed.
68%
Right products found, ranked too low
#6
Avg. best result position
56%
Best result outside top 3
29%
Irrelevant #1 result
Most severe example A customer searching "Dopp" gets zero results — a blank page, nothing at all.
Pattern Calibration Map

Where each query family needs tuning

These are diagnostic probes, not grades. Each row shows the strength of the tuning signal in that sample, separating severe shopper-visible misses from minor ranking polish.

Product Discovery
Direct match
Exact-match polish
4 probes reviewed: 4 visible misses.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “Bergs Potter” — The highest relevance result 'Vase Belle' (0.359 relevance) was buried at position #10, while lower-scoring results like 'Københavner Pflanztopf mit…
Can customers find an exact product when they already know the name?
Next tuning move: Protect exact product-title matches and verify they are not outranked by broader keyword matches.

Broad category
Collection routing

Does search understand high-level shopping terms and collection-style queries?
Next tuning move: Map common retail terms to collections, category pages, and merchandising rules.
Typo Tolerance
Typo
Typo recovery

Does search recover when brand or product terms are misspelled?
Next tuning move: Add fuzzy matching for high-value brands, product names, and common category terms.

Typo Tolerance
Partial query
Autocomplete intent

Do incomplete typed-ahead queries surface plausible products before the full term is entered?

Brand & Model Search
Brand search
Brand protection

Can search keep multiple requirements active at the same time?
Next tuning move: Score each constraint independently so one matched term does not drown out the rest.

Advanced Diagnostics

What the visible result set reveals

These checks stay inside the same query → results → audit boundary, but look beyond the single best result.

Result Set Purity

This checks how much of the visible result set is actually relevant, not just whether one good result exists somewhere. A noisy top 5 means customers must visually filter around weak results even when search technically found a match.

22%
top 5 relevant

Result

The visible result set is noisy: only 22% of top 5 results were relevant on average, and top 10 purity was 19%.

Evidence

  • "Bergs Potter": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "Küche": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "Wohnen": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "Haushalt": 0/5 relevant in top 5

How to remedy it

Tune ranking to demote low-relevance partial matches once a strong intent match exists. Add a top-N quality guardrail: for each important query class, require a minimum share of relevant results in the top 5, not only a good best-result position.

Top Result Trust

This asks whether the first result is defensible as the best thing to show a customer. A bad #1 is the clearest search failure because it shapes the shopper's first impression.

77%
questionable #1 results

Result

The first result is not reliable enough: 46 of 60 queries (77%) had a weak, partial, or clearly beaten #1 result.

Evidence

  • "Dopp": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "Herbst Vogelschutz": #1 was "Fleur de Sel aus Guérande" (score 0.26). The first result is weakly related to the query.
  • "Haushalt": #1 was "Sneakerbürste" (score 0.26). The first result is weakly related to the query.
  • "körperpfl produkte": #1 was "Seifenablage Confetti, Eckig" (score 0.27). The first result is weakly related to the query.

How to remedy it

Add a stricter first-result threshold for high-intent queries. If the top result is weak and a stronger result exists lower, boost the stronger exact/category/brand match or suppress the weak partial match from rank #1.

Attribute Drift

This decomposes multi-attribute queries to see which terms survive into the top results. It distinguishes right-category-but-wrong-attribute failures from total search failures.

4
queries with attribute loss

Result

4 multi-attribute queries showed some attribute loss in the top 5. The audit separates preserved, partially preserved, and dropped terms so the team can see which part of the query is being lost.

Evidence

  • "CHICO bunte Hängematten outdoor": dropped chico, bunte, ngematten, outdoor; partially preserved none
  • "Fermob blaue Gartenstühle": dropped blaue, gartenst, hle; partially preserved fermob
  • "Bergs Potter große Töpfe": dropped berg, potter, pfe; partially preserved gro
  • "Schwegler Holz Vogelhaus klein": dropped schwegler, vogelhau, klein; partially preserved none

How to remedy it

Extract query attributes before ranking and give required attributes explicit weight. For brand, size, material, activity, and style terms, penalize results that match the product type but omit or contradict the attribute.

Diversity vs Redundancy

This checks whether broad or exploratory queries show useful choice, or whether the top results are flooded by repeated variants. It is most useful for multi-brand or broad-catalog ecommerce, and less useful for true single-brand stores.

5
unique families in worst broad top 10

Result

The most redundant broad result set was "Töpfe": 5 unique product families across 10 top results.

Evidence

  • "Töpfe": 5 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "Koffer": 6 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "Haushalt": 9 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "Garten": 10 unique product families across 10 top results

How to remedy it

Group color/size/variant duplicates under a product-family cap for broad queries. Keep exact product searches variant-rich, but diversify broad discovery queries across families, brands, styles, and use cases.

Recommendations

Prioritized Roadmap

1

Implement brand name boosting for exact brand matches

This fixes Product Discovery and Brand & Model Search by ensuring products containing the exact brand name in queries rank at the top regardless of other factors.

Critical Quick Win
2

Configure search to treat brand names as mandatory filters

This fixes Brand & Model Search and Filters & Constraints by preventing brand bleed where generic products appear when customers specify a brand.

Critical Medium Effort
3

Add fuzzy string matching for single-character typos in brand names

This fixes Typo Tolerance by helping customers find Fermob when they type "Fermb" or Berger when they type "Bergs."

Critical Medium Effort
4

Implement semantic understanding for German use-case queries

This fixes Shopping Context by recognizing that "gemütlich machen" means creating cozy atmosphere and "füttern" implies need for feeding equipment.

Critical Major Project
5

Add category-aware boosting for broad category searches

This fixes Product Discovery by ensuring household items rank above sneaker products when customers search "Haushalt."

Critical Medium Effort
6

Configure synonym expansion for German product terms

This fixes Language Understanding by recognizing "Outdoor Möbel" as equivalent to "Gartenmöbel" and "Vogelhaus" as related to "Vogelfutterhaus."

Critical Medium Effort
7

Implement abbreviation expansion for common German terms

This fixes Brand & Model Search by expanding "körperpfl" to "Körperpflege" and "metallw" to "Metallwerke."

Critical Quick Win
8

Add compound word splitting for German search terms

This fixes Typo Tolerance by properly handling merged words like "gartenmöbel" and split words like "Garten möbel."

Critical Medium Effort
Industry Context

How You Compare

Metric Your Score Industry Target Source
Relevant result in top 3 44% 80%+ Baymard Institute
Average best result position #6 #1–2 Industry best practice
Irrelevant #1 result 29% <10% Industry best practice

70% of ecommerce sites cannot handle typos in their site search (Baymard Institute). Sites that do handle them gain a significant edge.

~30% of ecommerce visitors use site search, but they generate a disproportionate share of revenue — search users convert at 1.8–3x the rate of browse-only visitors (Econsultancy, Algolia).

31% of site searches end at a dead end — irrelevant results or zero results — across the average ecommerce site (Baymard Institute).

The top 3 results capture ~55–60% of all clicks. Results below position 3 are seen by a rapidly shrinking share of customers.

Revenue-Risk Failures

The queries most likely to cost sales

These examples show where the customer either sees no useful result, sees the wrong intent first, or has to dig past weaker results to find the right product.

#1 Dopp Zero results

The search returned zero results — the customer reached a dead end.

Evidence

Query 'Dopp' returned zero results despite this being a partial match for the Doppler brand name. The search engine failed to retrieve any products when it should have found Doppler-branded items through prefix matching.

Fix

Add query rewriting, token splitting, and fallback matching for this query pattern so customers do not hit a dead end.

What the customer got

No results

An empty results page — nothing to click. The shopper hits a dead end and most leave.

#2 Geschenke Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #15, after weaker results.

Evidence

The most relevant result 'PDF-Geschenkgutschein, Die guten Dinge' (relevance: 0.672) was buried at position #15, while less relevant items like 'Mokkakännchen Kupfer' (relevance: 0.475) and 'Milchkännchen Kupfer' (relevance: 0.479) occupied the top positions. The gift voucher, which directly matches the 'Geschenke' (gifts) query, was displaced by 14 positions.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Customer saw

#1 0.47

Mokkakännchen Kupfer 69,90 €

#2 0.48

Milchkännchen Kupfer 69,90 €

#3 0.49

Italienische Espressotassen Edelstahl (2 Stück) 54,90 €

#4 0.51

Italienische Cappuccino-Tassen Edelstahl (2 Stück) 69,90 €

#5 0.46

Allzweckmesser HAPPY TRADI 79,90 €

Should have seen

#15 0.67

PDF-Geschenkgutschein, Die guten Dinge

#4 0.51

Italienische Cappuccino-Tassen Edelstahl (2 Stück) 69,90 €

#14 0.49

Grubentuch Zwirnfrottier, Bunt 8,90 €

#3 0.49

Italienische Espressotassen Edelstahl (2 Stück) 54,90 €

#2 0.48

Milchkännchen Kupfer 69,90 €

#3 Garten Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #14, after weaker results.

Evidence

The two most relevant results 'Gartenkugel Keramik, Rot-Gelb' (0.664 relevance) and 'Gartenkugel Keramik, Weiß gesprenkelt' (0.633 relevance) were buried at positions #14 and #15, while a much less relevant 'Regentonne mit Sockel' (0.354 relevance) was ranked #1. The best matching result was displaced by 13 positions.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#4 Sommer Gartenmöbel Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #14, after weaker results.

Evidence

The highest relevance result 'Tisch Estoril, RAL 6009 Tannengrün' (0.449 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower relevance chair variants dominated the top positions. The second-best result 'Tisch Estoril, RAL 3013 Tomatenrot' (0.428 relevance) was at position #12. Meanwhile, position #1 showed 'Stuhl Estoril, RAL 3013 Tomatenrot' with only 0.400 relevance, and position #2 had the lowest relevance result 'Stuhl Estoril, RAL 9005 Tiefschwarz' at 0.359.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#5 keram werkstätten Poor ranking

The best matching result appeared at position #14, after weaker results.

Evidence

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant ceramic products but ranked them poorly. The highest relevance score (0.393) belongs to 'Insektenstation Granitkeramik' which was buried at position #14, while the lowest scoring result 'Gartenbodenleuchte Keramik, Natur' (0.322) was ranked #1. The top 3 positions show products with relevance scores of 0.322, 0.332, and 0.355, while much more relevant results with scores of 0.393, 0.391, and 0.385 were ranked at positions #14, #15, and #11 respectively.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#2 0.33

#6 Fermb Gartenmöbel No fuzzy matching

The best matching result appeared at position #13, after weaker results.

Evidence

The query 'Fermb Gartenmöbel' contains a typo in the brand name 'Fermob' (missing 'o'). The only actual Fermob product 'Fermob Gartenbank Louisiane, Chili' with the highest relevance score (0.781) was buried at position #13, while irrelevant products like 'Stuhl Estoril' variants and 'Tisch und Bank BTB Platte' dominated the top positions with much lower relevance scores (0.344-0.410).

Fix

Add typo and fuzzy matching for high-value product, category, and brand terms so small spelling errors still reach the right products.

Tisch und Bank BTB Platte, RAL 2002 Blutorange 199,00 €

Detailed Analysis

Product Discovery

5 examples
"Garten"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #14

The two most relevant results 'Gartenkugel Keramik, Rot-Gelb' (0.664 relevance) and 'Gartenkugel Keramik, Weiß gesprenkelt' (0.633 relevance) were buried at positions #14 and #15, while a much less relevant 'Regentonne mit Sockel' (0.354 relevance) was ranked #1. The best matching result was displaced by 13 positions.

Customer Saw

  1. Regentonne mit Sockel (159,00 €)
  2. Glasblüten für den Garten, Groß 3 Stück (29,90 €)
  3. Glasblüten für den Garten, Klein 5 Stück (27,90 €)
  4. Stuhl Estoril, RAL 3013 Tomatenrot (129,00 €)
  5. Stuhl Estoril, RAL 9005 Tiefschwarz (129,00 €)

Should Have Seen

  1. Gartenkugel Keramik, Rot-Gelb (19,90 €)
  2. Gartenkugel Keramik, Weiß gesprenkelt (19,90 €)
  3. Glasblüten für den Garten, Groß 3 Stück (29,90 €)

Best match was buried at position #14.

"Haushalt"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #12

The search correctly retrieved household products but ranked them terribly. The best household item 'Abfallsammler Ecobox' (relevance 0.447) was buried at position #12, while completely irrelevant sneaker products dominated positions #1-3: 'Sneakerbürste' (0.258), 'Sneaker Protector' (0.299), and 'Sneaker Refresher' (0.301). Other legitimate household items like 'Abtropfgestell Edelstahl Klein' (0.410) and 'Deckeleimer Emaille' (0.410) were also pushed down to positions #7 and #14 respectively.

Customer Saw

  1. Sneakerbürste (7,90 €)
  2. Sneaker Protector (17,90 €)
  3. Sneaker Refresher (15,90 €)
  4. Ersatzkopf Premium Grillbürste (13,90 €)
  5. Premium Grillbürste (37,90 €)

Should Have Seen

  1. Abfallsammler Ecobox (239,00 €)
  2. Abtropfgestell Edelstahl, Klein (159,00 €)
  3. Deckeleimer Emaille 3 Liter, Rot (44,90 €)

Best match was buried at position #12.

"Bergs Potter"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #10

The highest relevance result 'Vase Belle' (0.359 relevance) was buried at position #10, while lower-scoring results like 'Københavner Pflanztopf mit Untersetzer, Groß' (0.326 relevance) and 'Hoff Übertopf mit Untersetzer, Rohes Grau' (0.268 relevance) appeared at positions #1 and #3 respectively. The search engine retrieved relevant Bergs Potter products but completely failed to rank them by their computed relevance scores.

Customer Saw

  1. Københavner Pflanztopf mit Untersetzer, Groß (94,90 €)
  2. Københavner Pflanztopf mit Untersetzer, Mittel (64,90 €)
  3. Hoff Übertopf mit Untersetzer, Rohes Grau (34,90 €)
  4. Københavner Pflanztopf (39,90 €)
  5. Pflanzenstab Folk, Hinto (27,90 €)

Should Have Seen

  1. Vase Belle (199,00 €)
  2. Københavner Pflanztopf mit Untersetzer, Groß (94,90 €)
  3. Københavner Pflanztopf mit Untersetzer, Mittel (64,90 €)

Best match was buried at position #10.

"Fermob Gartenmöbel"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #8

Typo Tolerance

5 examples
"Dopp"
Partial query · Critical · Best result at position #16

Query 'Dopp' returned zero results despite this being a partial match for the Doppler brand name. The search engine failed to retrieve any products when it should have found Doppler-branded items through prefix matching.

No results returned — the customer saw an empty results page.

Best match was buried at position #16.

  1. Fermob Französischer Bistrostuhl, Acapulcoblau (85,90 €)
"Bergs Potter große Töpfe"
Multi-attribute · Pass · Best result at position #1

The query 'Bergs Potter große Töpfe' returned 1 result: 'Københavner Pflanztopf mit Untersetzer, Groß' at position #1 with relevance 0.410. This result correctly matches the size attribute 'große/Groß' (large) and is a pottery planter, which aligns with the product type requested.

Customer Saw

  1. Københavner Pflanztopf mit Untersetzer, Groß (94,90 €)

Should Have Seen

  1. Københavner Pflanztopf mit Untersetzer, Groß (94,90 €)
Positive Findings

What's Working

  • Retrieval is working — relevant results were found in 20 of 60 queries. The primary issue is ranking, not finding: the right results exist in the result set but aren't being surfaced at the top.
  • Close to correct on 21 queries ("Schwegler Vogel- u. Naturschutzprodukte", "Bergs Poter", "Schwelger Vogelschutz"): the best result was within the top 3, just not at #1.

Scope and Boundaries

What this audit covers

Included

Search relevance, ranking quality, retrieval gaps, query interpretation failures, visible result ordering, and prioritized fixes based on the tested query set.

Not included

Implementation work, platform configuration changes, merchandising strategy, analytics instrumentation, or a full conversion-rate analysis beyond the search result experience.

Next Steps

We'd welcome the opportunity to walk you through these findings in detail and discuss a remediation plan tailored to your team's priorities and timeline.

The issues identified in this audit are fixable — most ecommerce search platforms support the capabilities tested here, and the roadmap above is ordered by impact to help you prioritize.

Schedule a 30-Minute Walkthrough
